stories.title: Bellabu Bear Children's Sherpa Robes Recalled — Burn Hazard (1,300 Units)

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: RECALL: ~1,300 Bellabu Bear children's sherpa robes recalled for burn hazard. PAW Patrol, Minecraft, Minions, Batman prints — sizes S/M and M/L. Sold Jan 2024–Jul 2025 ~$60. Stop use now. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: Bellabu Bear sherpa robe recall: check neck label for "BB-NW12109." Burn hazard — fails flammability standard. Full refund available. Call 888-703-7752 or email help@bellabubear.com. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
**Window:** 8:00 AM MT

🚨 Colorado Springs parents — if your kids have a Bellabu Bear sherpa robe, please read this now.

About 1,300 Bellabu Bear children's bamboo sherpa robes have been recalled by the CPSC due to a burn hazard. The robes fail the mandatory flammability standards for children's sleepwear. The robes that are affected come in prints that will look very familiar: PAW Patrol, Minecraft, Minions, Batman, Justice League, Wonder Woman, Blaze and the Monster Machines, Trolls, Dora, as well as holiday and novelty prints including Nutcracker, hearts, Santa, stripes, bows, and dragons. They come in sizes S/M and M/L, and were sold for about $60 at Saks Fifth Avenue stores and boutiques nationwide, and online at Bellabubear.com, Letthembelittleboutique.com, and Saksfifthavenue.com from January 2024 through July 2025.

To identify a recalled robe, check the neck label for "Bellabu Bear," "BB-NW12109," and the size.

Stop using the robe immediately and contact Bellabu Bear for a full refund or store credit. They'll provide a prepaid shipping label so you can return it at no cost to you. Call 888-703-7752 (9 AM to 4 PM PT, Monday through Friday), email help@bellabubear.com, or visit bellabubear.com/pages/recall-childrens-sherpa-robes.

Do you have a Bellabu Bear robe at home — or know someone who might?

---
facebook.image_caption:
**Window:** 8:00 AM MT

CPSC recall alert: ~1,300 Bellabu Bear children's bamboo sherpa robes recalled for burn hazard. PAW Patrol, Minecraft, Minions, Batman and more character prints included. Sizes S/M and M/L. Stop use now — call 888-703-7752 or email help@bellabubear.com for a refund.

article.headline: Bellabu Bear Children's Sherpa Robes Recalled for Burn Hazard — PAW Patrol, Minecraft, Minions Prints Included

stories.title: D20 Leadership Change — Interim Superintendent Dr. Susan Field Named

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text: D20 update: Dr. Susan Field named interim superintendent through 2026-27. She has 19 years with D20 and is the current Asst. Superintendent for Learning Services. Board voted unanimously. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
**Window:** 10:00 AM MT

📋 D20 families — here's the update on Academy School District 20 leadership.

On May 15, the D20 Board of Education voted unanimously to fire Superintendent Jinger Haberer. The board cited insubordination and the unauthorized invitation of an undisclosed "person of interest" to a closed board meeting on May 7 — an act the board said compromised the district's attorney-client privilege.

On May 19, the board named Dr. Susan Field as interim superintendent for the 2026–27 school year. Dr. Field brings 19 years of experience inside D20, having started as principal of Woodmen-Roberts Elementary School and currently serving as Assistant Superintendent for Learning Services. The district sent a letter to families saying it "remains strong, stable and focused" through this leadership transition.

If your family is in D20 — schools like Pine Creek, Discovery Canyon, Rampart, Air Academy, and others — Dr. Field is the person now leading the district. She's expected to serve through the entirety of the 2026–27 school year.

D20 families: how are you feeling about the leadership change?

---
facebook.image_caption:
**Window:** 10:00 AM MT

D20 update: Dr. Susan Field has been named interim superintendent of Academy School District 20 through the 2026–27 school year, following the board's unanimous decision to fire Superintendent Haberer on May 15. Dr. Field has 19 years of experience with D20.

article.headline: D20 Names Dr. Susan Field as Interim Superintendent — What Colorado Springs Families Need to Know

stories.title: PPLD Summer Adventure 2026 — Sign Up June 1

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text: COS families: PPLD Summer Adventure starts June 1 — sign up at any library branch to earn prizes for reading. Free D11 lunch for anyone under 18 at East Library on weekdays this summer. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
**Window:** 10:00 AM MT

📚 The best free summer activity in Colorado Springs starts June 1 — and it comes with prizes AND free lunch.

The Pikes Peak Library District's Summer Adventure program kicks off June 1 at every PPLD branch across Colorado Springs. Sign up that day, start logging reading and activities, and earn prizes as you go. The program is for all ages — kids, teens, and adults. There are no fees and no registration required before June 1.

Here's the bonus: D11 is bringing its free lunch mobile unit to East Library every weekday in June and July. Any child or teen under 18 can stop in for lunch at no cost — no enrollment in D11 required, no income verification, nothing. It's just free.

Teens can also sign up to volunteer with the program and earn community service hours while helping others. Spots are limited and go quickly, so check ppld.org for details.

Which PPLD branch does your family use most?

---
facebook.image_caption:
**Window:** 10:00 AM MT

PPLD Summer Adventure 2026 kicks off June 1 at all Colorado Springs library branches — read, complete activities, earn prizes. Bonus: free D11 lunch at East Library weekdays in June and July for anyone under 18. Sign up June 1 at any branch.

article.headline: PPLD Summer Adventure 2026 Starts June 1 — Plus Free D11 Lunch at East Library

stories.title: Vevor Baby Swings Recalled — Suffocation Risk from Unsafe Incline

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text: RECALL: ~1,020 Vevor baby swings (BB501K, BB702A, BB005K) recalled for suffocation — violates Safe Sleep for Babies Act. Sold on Amazon/Vevor.com Jan–Aug 2025. Stop use now. Call 855-599-6320. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
**Window:** 12:00 PM MT

🚨 Parents of babies and toddlers: another swing recall to know about.

Sanven Technology has recalled about 1,020 Vevor baby swings due to a suffocation hazard. The swings have an incline angle greater than 10 degrees, which violates the mandatory federal safety standard for infant sleep products and the ban on inclined sleepers under the Safe Sleep for Babies Act. Babies placed in inclined sleepers at unsafe angles can have their chin fall to their chest, restricting their airway.

The three affected models are: BB501K (white and gray with a white base), BB702A (white and gray with a gray base), and BB005K (black and gray with a black base). They were sold on Vevor.com and Amazon from January 2025 through August 2025 for $65 to $80.

Stop using the swing immediately. To get your full refund, cut the swing cover in half, write "Recalled" with permanent marker on the swing's base, and send a photo to recalling@vevor.com. Then dispose of the swing. You can also call 855-599-6320 (9 AM to 5 PM PT, Monday through Friday).

Were you aware of the Safe Sleep for Babies Act restrictions on inclined sleepers?

---
facebook.image_caption:
**Window:** 12:00 PM MT

Recall: ~1,020 Vevor baby swings (models BB501K, BB702A, BB005K) recalled for suffocation hazard — unsafe incline violates Safe Sleep for Babies Act. Sold on Amazon and Vevor.com Jan–Aug 2025. Stop use and call 855-599-6320 for a full refund.

article.headline: Vevor Baby Swings Recalled for Suffocation Hazard — 1,020 Units Sold on Amazon

stories.title: Summer Is Here — Your COS Family Kick-Off Guide

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text: Summer is here, Colorado Springs. Pools are open, splash pads running. PPLD Summer Adventure and D49 free meals for kids ages 1-18 both start June 1. Your COS family summer begins now. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
**Window:** 3:00 PM MT

🌞 Memorial Day weekend is behind us, school is wrapping up, and summer is officially here for Colorado Springs families.

Here's what's open and what's coming up:

Right now: Monument Valley Pool (220 Mesa Rd, Mon–Sat 10 AM–6 PM, Sun 12–6 PM) and Wilson Ranch Pool (2335 Allegheny Dr, daily 10 AM–6 PM) are both open. Free splash pads at Panorama Park and Venezia Park Watering Hole are running daily 10 AM–6 PM. The Trackchair accessible hike program is running Wednesday through Sunday at Ute Valley Park, Red Rock Canyon, Blodgett, and Bluestem Prairie Open Space.

Starting June 1: PPLD Summer Adventure reading program launches — sign up at any library branch and earn prizes. D49 free meals begin for kids ages 1–18 (free breakfast and lunch, no income or D49 enrollment required through June 19). Deerfield Hills spray ground opens. D11's free lunch mobile unit starts at East Library weekdays.

Summer camps are open at Colorado Springs Conservatory (rock band, songwriting, theater, and more).

What's at the top of your family's Colorado Springs summer list?

---
facebook.image_caption:
**Window:** 3:00 PM MT

Summer is officially underway in Colorado Springs. Pools are open. Splash pads are running. PPLD Summer Adventure and D49 free meals for kids ages 1–18 both launch June 1. Here's your COS family summer guide.

article.headline: Your COS Summer Starts Now — What's Open and What's Coming for Colorado Springs Families
